description_long: This object is a cylindrical stone block with a dark, mottled gray surface, resembling a core sample or a structural test piece. The surface is rough and matte, with visible text markings in black ink, including numbers and letters, suggesting it is used for measurement or identification purposes. The block appears solid and dense, with no visible cracks or chips, and the top and bottom faces are flat and parallel, indicating it was likely cut or shaped with precision. The markings include dimensions and possibly sample identifiers, which are clearly legible on one side. The overall appearance suggests it is made from a dense stone material, such as concrete or basalt, commonly used in construction or civil engineering contexts. Its size and form make it suitable as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments, such as on sidewalks or in testing facilities.
description: A cylindrical, dark gray stone block with rough, matte texture and black ink markings, resembling a core sample or test piece. It is solid, dense, and features flat, parallel ends.
description_view_0: This view shows the side of the stone block with visible black ink markings, including numbers and letters, on a rough, dark gray surface.
description_view_1: This view displays a mostly unmarked, dark gray cylindrical surface, highlighting the block's solid and uniform texture.
description_view_2: This angle reveals additional faint markings and the consistent, rough stone texture around the cylinder.
description_view_3: This view prominently features the main set of black ink markings, including sample identifiers and dimensions, on the stone's surface.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or civil engineering test site
attribute_installation_method: freestanding or placed on flat ground
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears relatively clean and unaged.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Visible black ink markings with numbers, letters, and dimensions.
